q: Two angles are supplementary. If one angle measures 115°, what is the measure of the other angle?
explain: Supplementary angles sum to 180°. So the other angle is 180° − 115° = 65°.

q: A right triangle has legs of length 9 and 12. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
explain: This is a 3-4-5 triangle scaled by 3: legs 9 and 12 equal 3×3 and 3×4, so the hypotenuse is 3×5 = 15. Check: 9² + 12² = 81 + 144 = 225 = 15².

q: A circle has radius 5. What is its area?
A cube has edge length 4. What is its volume?
Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the eight angles formed measures 70°, and a second angle is co-interior (same-side interior) to it. What is the measure of that co-interior angle?
explain: Same-side (co-interior) interior angles formed by a transversal cutting parallel lines are supplementary. So the angle is 180° − 70° = 110°.

q: What is the positive difference between one interior angle of a regular hexagon and one interior angle of a regular pentagon?
explain: Interior angle of a regular n-gon = (n−2)·180°/n. Pentagon: (3·180)/5 = 540/5 = 108°. Hexagon: (4·180)/6 = 720/6 = 120°. Difference = 120° − 108° = 12°.

q: A circle has radius 6. A sector of this circle has a central angle of 60°. What is the area of the sector?
A right circular cylinder has radius 3 and height 7. Its volume is k·π. What is the value of k?
explain: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h = π(3²)(7) = π·9·7 = 63π. The coefficient of π is 63.

q: An isosceles right triangle (45-45-90) has each leg of length 8.
explain: In a 45-45-90 triangle the hypotenuse = leg·√2 = 8√2 ≈ 8(1.414) ≈ 11.31. Since 11.31 > 11, Quantity A is greater.

q: Two similar triangles have areas in the ratio 9 : 16. The perimeter of the smaller triangle is 21. What is the perimeter of the larger triangle?
explain: For similar figures, the ratio of areas equals the square of the ratio of corresponding lengths. Area ratio 9:16 gives length (and perimeter) ratio √9 : √16 = 3 : 4. So the larger perimeter = 21·(4/3) = 28.

q: A 30-60-90 right triangle has its shortest side (the side opposite the 30° angle) of length 6.
qa: The length of the side opposite the 60° angle
explain: In a 30-60-90 triangle the sides are in ratio 1 : √3 : 2 opposite the 30°, 60°, 90° angles respectively. The shortest side is 6, so the side opposite 60° = 6√3 ≈ 6(1.732) ≈ 10.39. Since 10.39 > 10, Quantity A is greater.

q: A triangle has two sides of length 7 and 10. Which of the following could be the length of the third side? Select all that apply.
explain: By the triangle inequality, the third side must be strictly greater than 10 − 7 = 3 and strictly less than 10 + 7 = 17. Valid values: 4, 8, 13, 16. Not valid: 2 (≤ 3) and 17 (= the sum, which gives a degenerate triangle).
